e: "torturar" is a Spanish word meaning "to torture, torment". The context shows how it's used: "un chico de 14 años de edad que fue torturado y asesinado" (a 14-year-old boy was tortured and killed).
"piscina" is a Spanish word meaning "swimming pool". The context shows how it's used: "yo nadaba en la piscina del patio" (I used to swim in the patio swimming pool).

"prestigioso" is a Spanish word meaning "prestigious". The context shows how it's used: "se fue a estudiar en un prestigioso conservatorio en Londres" (she went to study at a prestigious conservatory in London).
"sinfonía" is a Spanish word meaning "symphony". The context shows how it's used: "la primera sinfonía beethoveniana triunfó completamente" (Beethoven’s first symphony was a complete triumph).
"espina" is a Spanish word meaning "thorn, spine". The context shows how it's used: "no hay rosas sin espinas" (there are no roses without thorns).
"estatura" is a Spanish word meaning "stature, height". The context shows how it's used: "las modelos tienen que tener 1,7 m de estatura por lo menos" (models must be at least 1.7 m tall).
"fatiga" is a Spanish word meaning "fatigue". The context shows how it's used: "para aliviar de la fatiga de su pierna rota" (to alleviate the fatigue of her broken leg).

"parámetro" is a Spanish word meaning "parameter". The context shows how it's used: "son sistemas de parámetros imposibles de contrastar con la realidad" (they are systems with parameter that are impossible to compare with reality).

"compasión" is a Spanish word meaning "compassion". The context shows how it's used: "se establece la relación con amor, comprensión y compasión" (a relationship is based on love, understanding, and compassion).

"especulación" is a Spanish word meaning "speculation, conjecture". The context shows how it's used: "este año se caracterizó por la especulación y la incertidumbre" (this year is characterized by speculation and uncertainty).

s: "agachar" means "to duck, bend down" in Spanish
e: "agachar" is a Spanish word meaning "to duck, bend down". The context shows how it's used: "Tuve que agacharme y acercarme a los alumnos más pequeños." (I had to bend down and get closer to the smaller students.).

s: This Spanish adjective describes something that has not been damaged or altered in any way.
q: What Spanish word is related to "damaged"?
e: "intacto" is a Spanish word meaning "intact". The context shows how it's used: "el paquete llegó intacto a su destino" (the package arrived intact to its destination).

"pirámide" is a Spanish word meaning "pyramid". The context shows how it's used: "construyeron las pirámides y otras estructuras monumentales" (they built the pyramids and other monumental structures).

"vibración" is a Spanish word meaning "vibration". The context shows how it's used: "el sonido se produce por la vibración de las cuerdas vocales" (the sound is produced by the vibration of vocal chords).
"étnico" is a Spanish word meaning "ethnic". The context shows how it's used: "su clase social específica el origen étnico" (your social class indicates your ethnic origins).
"pésimo" is a Spanish word meaning "dreadful, awful". The context shows how it's used: "las trataban muy mal, sentí muy pésimo" (they were treated very badly, I felt awful).
"barroco" is a Spanish word meaning "baroque, elaborate". The context shows how it's used: "diseñaron un enorme complejo de estilo Barroco" (they designed an enormous complex in the baroque style).
"gala" is a Spanish word meaning "gala (performance/dinner)". The context shows how it's used: "será una gala a beneficio de la institución" (it will be a gala to benefit the institution).

"nobleza" is a Spanish word meaning "nobility, uprightness". The context shows how it's used: "la nobleza no se hereda, señor; la creamos con nuestros actos" (nobility is not inherited, sir; we create it with our actions).

"enormemente" is a Spanish word meaning "enormously". The context shows how it's used: "la ejecución técnica del proyecto es enormemente complicada" (the technical execution of the project is enormously complicated).

"provecho" is a Spanish word meaning "benefit, use". The context shows how it's used: "buscaban la forma de sacar provecho de la situación" (they are looking for ways to take advantage of the situation).

"cauce" is a Spanish word meaning "(river) bed, ditch". The context shows how it's used: "el río siempre irá por ese cauce, por ese valle" (the river will always go through this riverbed, through this valley).
"chispa" is a Spanish word meaning "spark". The context shows how it's used: "se le encendió una chispa en los ojos" (it lit a spark in his eyes).

"burro" is a Spanish word meaning "donkey". The context shows how it's used: "estar harto de trabajar como un burro bajo presiones constantes" (to be fed up of working like a donkey under constant strain).
"inundación" is a Spanish word meaning "flood, deluge". The context shows how it's used: "la inundación anual del río Nilo" (the yearly flood of the River Nile).
"sirena" is a Spanish word meaning "siren, mermaid". The context shows how it's used: "la sirena del barco no estaba funcionando" (the boat’s siren was not working).

"curiosamente" is a Spanish word meaning "strangely, funnily enough". The context shows how it's used: "pero, curiosamente, no se mencionó en absoluto dicho mensaje" (but, strangely enough, he did not mention the message at all).

"ofensiva" is a Spanish word meaning "offensive, attack". The context shows how it's used: "lanzó una exitosa ofensiva contra la ocupación española" (he launched a successful attack against the Spanish occupation).
"exótico" is a Spanish word meaning "exotic". The context shows how it's used: "el tráfico de animales exóticos es ilegal" (the trafficking of exotic animals is illegal).

"cima" is a Spanish word meaning "summit, peak, top". The context shows how it's used: "uno se debe sentir así cuando llega a la cima de una montaña" (one should feel like this when arriving at the top of a mountain).

"reconstrucción" is a Spanish word meaning "reconstruction". The context shows how it's used: "busqué materiales para la reconstrucción de mi casa" (I looked for materials for the rebuilding of my house).
"enmarcar" is a Spanish word meaning "to frame". The context shows how it's used: "para enmarcar esta situación en un contexto teórico" (to frame this situation and put it in a theoretical context).
"melancólico" is a Spanish word meaning "melancholy". The context shows how it's used: "encierra un espíritu poético, melancólico, sentimental" (she lived in a melancholy state).

"articulación" is a Spanish word meaning "articulation, joint". The context shows how it's used: "el poner en juego la articulación de las ideas básicas del psicoanálisis" (putting in play the interaction of the basic concepts from psychoanalysis).

"mediterráneo" is a Spanish word meaning "Mediterranean". The context shows how it's used: "algunos países mediterráneos—Grecia, Italia y España" (some Mediterranean countries—Greece, Italy, and Spain).

"retratar" is a Spanish word meaning "to portray, draw". The context shows how it's used: "ha logrado retratar al Ecuador desde el punto de vista de un turista" (he has managaed to portray Ecuador from the tourist’s viewpoint).

"flota" is a Spanish word meaning "fleet". The context shows how it's used: "Criticó que esta flota de vehículos tiene de 20 a 35 años." (He complained that this fleet of vehicles is 20–35 years old).
"fracción" is a Spanish word meaning "fraction". The context shows how it's used: "en la primera fracción de un segundo después del Big Bang" (in the first fraction of a second after the Big Bang).

"contagiar" is a Spanish word meaning "to infect, infest". The context shows how it's used: "un gato infectado puede contagiar la enfermedad a otros" (an infected cat can transmit the illness to others).

"molde" is a Spanish word meaning "mold, cast, pattern". The context shows how it's used: "el molde es una cavidad que tiene la forma exacta de un barco" (the mold is a hollow pan that has the exact shape of a boat).
"fauna" is a Spanish word meaning "fauna, wildlife". The context shows how it's used: "también encontramos gran variedad de fauna y flora" (we also found a wide variety of wildlife and plants).

s: "apasionar" means "to inspire, be passionate about" in Spanish
e: "apasionar" is a Spanish word meaning "to inspire, be passionate about". The context shows how it's used: "quiero apasionarme por la carrera y no aburrirme" (I want to be passionate about my career and not be bored).
"nalgas" is a Spanish word meaning "buttocks, backside, bottom". The context shows how it's used: "me veo demasiado gordo en las nalgas y en la piernas" (I look too fat in my backside and my legs).

"indignar" is a Spanish word meaning "to anger". The context shows how it's used: "espero que pueda resolverlo, en lugar de indignar e insultar" (I hope you can resolve it, instead of angering and insulting).
"convento" is a Spanish word meaning "convent, monastery". The context shows how it's used: "se fueron al convento a buscar unas monjas" (they went to the convent to look for some nuns).
"literalmente" is a Spanish word meaning "literally". The context shows how it's used: "hasta ser borrados literalmente del mapa" (to be literally wiped off the map).
"coco" is a Spanish word meaning "coconut". The context shows how it's used: "en el coco, la cáscara dura exterior es el pericarpio" (in the coconut, the outer hard shell is the pericarp).
"chimenea" is a Spanish word meaning "chimney, fireplace". The context shows how it's used: "mi casa ideal necesita tener una chimenea" (my ideal house must have a fireplace).

"supervivencia" is a Spanish word that translates to "survival". Example: "posibilita la supervivencia de los animales que todavía están vivos" (it enables the survival of the animals that are still alive)
"tentar" is a Spanish word that translates to "to tempt, entice". Example: "es un error muy grave tentar a la muerte" (tempting death is a very serious mistake)
"puramente" is a Spanish word that translates to "purely, simply". Example: "Lo hizo de fines puramente superficiales." (He did it for purely superficial reasons.)
"milenio" is a Spanish word that translates to "millennium". Example: "fue parte de Egipto durante el segundo milenio antes de nuestra era" (it was part of Egypt during 2000 BC)
"lomo" is a Spanish word that translates to "back, loin, spine". Example: "viaja al lomo de un burro" (she travels on the back of a donkey)
"glorioso" is a Spanish word that translates to "glorious". Example: "culminó un momento glorioso de nuestro pasado" (it culminated in a glorious moment of our past)
"hazaña" is a Spanish word that translates to "deed, heroic feat". Example: "la hija del héroe cuenta hazañas increíbles" (the hero’s daughter tells of incredible deeds)

"irlandés" is a Spanish word that translates to "Irish". Example: "el gobierno irlandés no envió ningún representante a su funeral" (the Irish government didn’t send a single representative to his funeral)

"fugaz" is a Spanish word that translates to "fleeting, brief". Example: "la felicidad es una sensación de completa satisfacción medio fugaz" (happiness is a fleeting feeling of complete satisfaction)
"adversario" is a Spanish word that translates to "adversary". Example: "Está atacando al adversario en su punto débil." (He is attacking the adversary at his weak point)
"diámetro" is a Spanish word that translates to "diameter". Example: "se compone de dos tubos de 8 metros de diámetro" (it is made of two tubes of 8 meters in diameter)
"descifrar" is a Spanish word that translates to "to decipher". Example: "para descifrar un fragmento escrito en caracteres cuneiformes" (to decipher a fragment written in cuneiform characters)
"medianoche" is a Spanish word that translates to "midnight". Example: "A partir de las 20.00 y hasta la medianoche" (From 8 p.m. until midnight)
"encarar" is a Spanish word that translates to "to face, confront". Example: "salió sin saber ya cómo encarar el asunto" (she left without knowing how to face the issue)

"discreto" is a Spanish word that translates to "discreet, tactful". Example: "debíamos ser discretas y nunca tener contacto con los clientes" (we had to be discreet and never come in contact with the clients)

s: "fiscalía" means 'office of the public prosecutor' in Spanish
e: "fiscalía" is a Spanish word that translates to "office of the public prosecutor". Example: "no se ha aceptado la decisión de la Fiscalía" (the district attorney’s decision has not been accepted)
"natal" is a Spanish word that translates to "native, birth". Example: "estudió Derecho en su ciudad natal" (he studied Law in his native city)
"prolongado" is a Spanish word that translates to "prolonged, lengthy". Example: "antes de saludarse con palabras se dieron un abrazo prolongado" (before saying hello they gave each other a big hug)
"negativa" is a Spanish word that translates to "negative, denial". Example: "no acepto una negativa" (I won’t accept a denial)
"asombroso" is a Spanish word that translates to "amazing". Example: "¿Hay algo que este asombroso artista no sea capaz de hacer?" (Is there something that this amazing artist is not able to do?)
"coherente" is a Spanish word that translates to "coherent, connected". Example: "todavía no era una presentación coherente y ordenada" (it still wasn’t a coherent and organized presentation)

"potenciar" is a Spanish word that translates to "to strengthen, boost". Example: "una cantante africana, muy activa en potenciar la educación de mujeres" (an African singer, very active in promoting women’s education)
"segmento" is a Spanish word that translates to "segment, sector". Example: "representan un segmento grande de la población" (they represent a large segment of the population)

"contundente" is a Spanish word that translates to "decisive, blunt, forceful". Example: "el objetivo era derrotarlos de forma rápida y contundente" (the object was to defeat them in a quick and forceful way)
"ingenuo" is a Spanish word that translates to "naïve, ingenuous". Example: "después de tantos años, ya no tiene derecho a ser ingenuo" (after so many years, he has no right to be naïve)

"minúsculo" is a Spanish word that translates to "minute, insignificant". Example: "estaba concentrada en un punto minúsculo, menor que un átomo" (she was focused on a tiny, minute dot, smaller than an atom)

s: This Spanish noun refers to an official who examines or investigates something or someone.
q: What Spanish word is related to "Thinking"?
e: "inspector" is a Spanish word that translates to "inspector". Example: "apareció un inspector municipal que quiso cobrar impuestos" (a town inspector showed up and he wanted to collect taxes)
"auge" is a Spanish word that translates to "peak, boom". Example: "por estas medidas el gobierno que han frenado el auge económico" (by these means the government has stopped the economic boom)
"torpe" is a Spanish word that translates to "clumsy, awkward, slow". Example: "soy un poco torpe para las matemáticas" (I am pretty slow in math)
"pureza" is a Spanish word that translates to "purity, chastity". Example: "el blanco representa la pureza y inocencia" (the color white represents purity and innocence)
"ramo" is a Spanish word that translates to "bunch (of flowers), branch". Example: "le regalé un ramo de rosas blancas" (I gave her a bouquet of white roses)

"auditorio" is a Spanish word that translates to "hearing, auditorium". Example: "Hubo un extraordinario aplauso desde cada rincón de el auditorio." (There was an extraordinary applause coming from every corner of the auditorium.)
"tregua" is a Spanish word that translates to "truce, rest". Example: "su mundo duro era sin tregua ni perdón" (her difficult world had no rest or forgiveness)

"escalón" is a Spanish word that translates to "stair, rung". Example: "una especie de escalera donde no se puede pasar al segundo escalón" (the kind of stairway where you can’t get to the second step)
"duque" is a Spanish word that translates to "duke". Example: "Inés vivió su infancia en la palacio del duque" (Inez lived her childhood in the duke’s palace)

"impecable" is a Spanish word that translates to "impeccable, faultless". Example: "la mujer tenía un gusto impecable y un extraordinario discernimiento" (the woman had impeccable taste and an extraordinary discernment)

"amparar" is a Spanish word that translates to "to protect, shelter". Example: "protestó el fascismo amparado y financiado por Estados Unidos" (he protested the fascism that was supported and financed by the United States)
"esencialmente" is a Spanish word that translates to "essentially". Example: "creen que la naturaleza humana es esencialmente buena" (they believe that human nature is essentially good)

"inminente" is a Spanish word that translates to "imminent". Example: "el bolchevique creía en la extensión de la revolución soviética" (the Bolshevik believed in the imminent extension of the Soviet revolution)

"jurisdicción" is a Spanish word that translates to "jurisdiction". Example: "Macao, territorio de China que fue jurisdicción portuguesa hasta 1999" (Macao, Chinese territory that was under Portuguese jurisdiction until 1999)

"monopolio" is a Spanish word that translates to "monopoly". Example: "El monopolio comercial era tan rígido que impedía el comercio libre." (The commercial monopoly was so strong that it interfered with free trade.)
"razonamiento" is a Spanish word that translates to "reasoning". Example: "no existía un razonamiento capaz de disuadirle" (there was no reasoning that could dissuade him)
"ofensivo" is a Spanish word that translates to "offensive, aggressive". Example: "No se permitirán comentarios ofensivos o contrarios." (Offensive or contradictory comments are not permitted.)
"áspero" is a Spanish word that translates to "rough, coarse, harsh". Example: "hablaba el gitano con áspero acento" (the gypsy spoke with a harsh accent)

"rentable" is a Spanish word that translates to "profitable". Example: "tendría que cobrar al menos 30.000€ para que me salga rentable" (I would have to charge at least €30,000 in order for it to be profitable)
"navegador" is a Spanish word that translates to "browser, navigator". Example: "asi todo lo que hace lo hace a través de un navegador web" (so everything he does is through a web browser)

"tradicionalmente" is a Spanish word that translates to "traditionally". Example: "tradicionalmente este trabajo ha sido desempeñado por los hombres" (traditionally this work has been carried out by men)
"alusión" is a Spanish word that translates to "allusion, hint". Example: "hacía alusión a las supersticiones del viejo paisano" (he alluded to the superstitions of the old peasant)
"excursión" is a Spanish word that translates to "excursion". Example: "participaban en una excursión para observar aves migratorias" (they participated in an excursion to watch migratory birds)
"estudiantil" is a Spanish word that translates to "student". Example: "contamos con una población cercana a los 15 mil estudiantes" (we are counting on a population of around 15 thousand students)

Ilustre is an adjective describing someone or something of great distinction and fame. It derives from Latin 'illustris' meaning 'bright' or 'distinguished.' Plato famously followed his renowned master Socrates.

Permanentemente is an adverb derived from 'permanente' (permanent). It indicates an action or state that continues without end or for an indefinite duration. The prefix 'perm-' relates to 'perma' meaning lasting or enduring.
